sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check what changed after pressing RIGHT. I need to compare the before and after frames to see if the character moved or if something else changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after RIGHT
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation

def get_node_info(seg):
    info = {}
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        b = n['boundary']
        if b:
            rows = [p[0] for p in b]
            cols = [p[1] for p in b]
            info[n['id']] = {
                'color': n['color'],
                'pixels': n['pixels'],
                'hash': n['hash'],
                'bbox': (min(rows), min(cols), max(rows), max(cols))
            }
    return info

before_info = get_node_info(before)
after_info = get_node_info(after)

print("=== Changes detected ===")
all_ids = set(list(before_info.keys()) + list(after_info.keys()))
for iid in sorted(all_ids):
    b = before_info.get(iid)
    a = after_info.get(iid)
    if b != a:
        print(f"  Node {iid}: {b} -> {a}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The node IDs shifted around, but I can track objects by their hashes. Let me trace what happened:
- Black shape (hash `abb5f60543a63746`): was at (15,18)-(23,26), now at (15,21)-(23,29) → moved right by 3 columns
- Dark gray shape (hash `cbd65249d8aabbbe`): was at (15,36)-(23,44), now at (15,33)-(23,41) → moved left by 3 columns  
- Yellow shape (hash `8318ba1b15e9d6b0`): stayed at (45,51)-(53,59) → unchanged
- White dots (hash `a57e9c94faa522e9`): moved with the black shape
So pressing RIGHT moved the black character (with white dots) 3 cells to the right AND moved the dark gray shape 3 cells to the left. The yellow shape didn't move.
Wait, let me re-examine. The black shape moved from col 18-26 to 21-29 (right by 3). The dark gray shape moved from col 36-44 to 33-41 (left by 3). They're moving toward each other!
The sky blue divider is at cols 30-32. The black shape is now at 21-29 (right edge at 29, just before the divider at 30). The dark gray shape is now at 33-41 (left edge at 33, just after the divider at 32).
It seems like the character moved toward the divider. Maybe the goal is to move the character to collide with or pass through the divider?
Let me try pressing RIGHT again to see if it can cross the divider.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try RIGHT again to see if character can cross the divider
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
